в”‚ cx, cy = vp.get('centerX', 6572), vp.get('centerY', 5224)вЂ¦
  в”” 20 lines...

в—Џ Operation cancelled by user

вќЇ Why is it always calling python? Should not be a requirement to position stickies

в—Џ Good point вЂ” the batch API should accept the positions directly. No Python needed.
  Let me just fire it:

в—Џ Create 5 stickies + connectors in one batch (shell)
  в”‚ curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:1236/_storyboard/canvas/batch" \
  в”‚ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  в”‚ -d '{
  в”‚ "name": "viewfinder-redesign",
  в”‚ "operations": [вЂ¦
  в”” 27 lines...

в—Џ 5 stickies + 5 connectors created in one request. No Python, no loops вЂ” just a single
  JSON payload with $0вЂ“$4 refs.

  Your point stands though: the earlier attempt used Python because I needed to compute
  circular positions. For simple layouts like a column, it's just hardcoded coordinates
  in the JSON. The batch API itself doesn't require any scripting language.

 ~/workspace/st... [вЋ‡ 4.3.0--prompt-widget*%]
